rationale:
Intuition: The home screen chores widget shows a progress bar per person but no individual items, and there's no way to tap through to a filtered chore list. The data already exists — /api/chores?person=X&when=today returns full items, and /chores renders a full grid — but the two aren't connected and the widget is display-only.

What I'd fix: Three gaps: (1) /api/today returns only summary counts, not the item list needed to render inline chores; (2) the /chores page has no per-person filter so a link from the widget would dump all persons; (3) the home widget has no item list and no clickable link.

How: 
- data_api.py: enrich the /api/today query to join chore rows and return items:[{id, chore_name, done}] nested under each person entry alongside the existing counts.
- hub.py + chores.html: accept an optional ?person=X query param on GET /chores; if present, filter the persons list to only that person so the page shows one column.
- home.html choresWidget: wrap each person card in an anchor tag pointing to /chores?person={name}; below the progress bar render a small ul of today's items with a checkmark or open circle prefix based on done status. Keep the card compact — truncate to ~4 items with a "…and N more" tail if the list is long.
instructions:
Files to touch: middleware/data_api.py, middleware/hub.py, templates/chores.html, templates/home.html.

1. middleware/data_api.py — enrich /api/today
   - Find the route that returns the per-person today summary (look for the /api/today endpoint handler).
   - The current query fetches summary counts (open, done_count) grouped by person.
   - Change it to also fetch the individual chore rows for today per person. Either do a second query keyed on the same persons list, or rewrite as a single query that fetches all today rows and aggregates in Python.
   - Shape each person object as: { "person": str, "open": int, "done_count": int, "items": [ {"id": int, "chore_name": str, "done": bool}, ... ] }
   - Items should be today's chores only (same filter logic already used for counts). Order by done asc, then chore_name asc so undone items float to the top.
   - Do not break the existing count fields — the widget already uses them.

2. middleware/hub.py — add ?person= filter to /chores
   - Find the GET /chores route handler.
   - Add: person_filter = request.args.get("person", None)
   - When building the persons list (or querying chores per person), if person_filter is set, narrow to only that person (case-insensitive match is fine).
   - Pass person_filter into the template context as "person_filter" so the template can conditionally show a back link.
   - No other changes to the route logic.

3. templates/chores.html — back link when filtered
   - At the top of the page body (above the chores grid), add a conditional block: if person_filter is set, render a small "← All chores" link pointing to /chores.
   - No other structural changes to the chores page.

4. templates/home.html — redesign choresWidget
   - Find the choresWidget JS/HTML block that renders the per-person cards.
   - Wrap each person card's outer element in an <a href="/chores?person={name}"> tag (or set it as an onclick that navigates). The entire card should be clickable, not just a button inside it.
   - Below the existing progress bar inside each card, add a <ul> that iterates over item in person.items:
       - Each <li> shows: a checkmark emoji (✓) if done, an open circle (○) if not, followed by the chore_name.
       - Done items should be visually de-emphasized (e.g. text-muted class or line-through style).
   - Cap the rendered list at 4 items. If there are more than 4, add a final <li> styled as muted: "…and N more" where N is the remainder.
   - If items is empty (person has no chores today), show a small "All clear" note instead of an empty list.
   - Preserve all existing card styling (badge, progress bar, colors). The item list is additive below the bar.
   - Make sure the anchor wrapping doesn't break any existing JS event handlers on the card — use pointer-events carefully if needed, or make only the card header/title the anchor and the list non-linked.
